file_insights.purpose: QR code generation and management service
file_insights.architecture: Service layer with dependency injection, CRUD operations for QR code management

patterns_discovered.description: Uses @injectable() and @inject() decorators for IoC container integration with tsyringe
All operations validate organizationId as tenant isolation mechanism
Uses custom AppException class for standardized error responses (notFound, alreadyExists)
Emits domain events via EventService for operations like qrcode.created, qrcode.regenerated
Implements paginated queries with boundary validation (normalize page, clamp perPage)
Uses env module and process.env for upload directories, public URLs, and app domain
Async file operations for saving generated QR code images to disk
Generates cryptographically secure unique codes for QR code identification

gotchas_discovered.description: Service enforces only one active QR code per table; attempting to generate another throws alreadyExists exception
QR code URLs depend on menu slug existence; missing menu causes NotFound exception
Page and perPage parameters are normalized; invalid values are clamped rather than rejected
QR code images are saved to file system; regeneration leaves old images in place (potential orphaning)
Public URLs switch between http and https based on NODE_ENV; protocol mismatch risk in mixed environments

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Scan count immutability in read path
gotchas_discovered.description: recordScan method increments scansCount but does not return updated entity; caller must refetch if updated count needed

approach_outcome.implementation_scope: Full-featured QR code service with generation, regeneration, activation, deactivation, and scan tracking
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - comprehensive documentation, type safety, error handling, event emission
approach_outcome.completeness: Complete - includes CRUD operations, pagination, tenant isolation, image storage
approach_outcome.complexity: High - 998 lines with multiple responsibilities (generation, storage, lifecycle management)
approach_outcome.test_coverage: Not evident from commit - likely requires integration tests for image generation and file operations

recommendations.recommendation: Implement image cleanup strategy for regenerated QR codes to prevent orphaned files from accumulating
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation saves new images without removing old ones during regeneration

recommendations.recommendation: Add validation for QRCodeGenerationOptions to prevent invalid configurations (e.g., width < minimum)
recommendations.rationale: No apparent validation of generation options before passing to qrcode library

recommendations.recommendation: Consider caching frequently accessed QR codes or precomputing expiration checks
recommendations.rationale: Service includes expiration date support but no visible cleanup or optimization for expired codes

recommendations.recommendation: Add comprehensive test suite covering image generation, file I/O, tenant isolation, and concurrent operations
recommendations.rationale: Service has multiple external dependencies (file system, crypto) requiring isolated testing

recommendations.recommendation: Document QR code expiration lifecycle and manual cleanup procedures
recommendations.rationale: expiresAt field exists but behavior for expired codes is not documented

recommendations.recommendation: Validate URL parameters (table ID, code) to prevent injection attacks in generated QR code URLs
recommendations.rationale: User-provided data is embedded in publicUrl without apparent sanitization
